# fz

> fz (PyPI: funz-fz) is a Python framework for parametric scientific computing: it wraps
> any simulation code that reads input files and writes output files, runs parameter
> studies in parallel (local shell, SSH, SLURM, legacy Funz servers), and parses results
> into pandas DataFrames. Six core functions, each with a CLI twin: fzi (parse input
> variables), fzc (compile inputs), fzo (parse outputs), fzr (run parametric study),
> fzl (list models/calculators), fzd (adaptive design of experiments).

Key concepts: input files are parameterized with `$var` markers and `@{formula}`
expressions; a *model* (JSON/dict) defines the parsing syntax and shell commands that
extract outputs; *calculators* (URIs like `sh://cmd`, `ssh://user@host/cmd`,
`slurm://:partition/cmd`, `cache://path`) define where cases run.

## Reference

- [Condensed API/CLI reference](skills/fz/reference.md): function signatures, model and
  calculator JSON schemas, calculator URI grammar, environment variables, input syntax
- [Agent workflow guide](skills/fz/SKILL.md): step-by-step process to wrap a simulation
  code and run a study, with verification at each step
- [Custom fzd algorithms](skills/fz/algorithms.md): interface for adaptive
  design-of-experiments / optimization algorithms

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): complete user guide with examples
- [Core functions](context/core-functions.md): fzi, fzc, fzo, fzr, fzl, fzd in detail
- [Input syntax guide](context/syntax-guide.md): variables, formulas, defaults
- [Model definition](context/model-definition.md): model structure, aliases, output parsing
- [Calculators](context/calculators.md): calculator types, URIs, configuration
- [Formulas and interpreters](context/formulas-and-interpreters.md): Python/R formula evaluation
- [Parallelism and caching](context/parallel-and-caching.md): performance strategies
- [Quick examples](context/quick-examples.md): common usage patterns

## Examples

- [Examples overview](examples/examples.md): example models and studies
- [fzd examples](examples/fzd_example.md): design of experiments walkthroughs
- [DataFrame input](examples/dataframe_input.md): non-factorial designs

## Optional

- [Release notes](NEWS.md)
- [Funz protocol](context/funz-protocol.md): legacy Java Funz server protocol
- [Shell path configuration](context/shell-path.md): bash on Windows (FZ_SHELL_PATH)
